title: CHB-MIT
description: The CHB-MIT Scalp EEG Database is a collection of continuous EEG recordings from 22 pediatric subjects with intractable epilepsy, acquired at Children's Hospital Boston. The dataset comprises 686 EEG recordings with 198 annotated seizure events, collected during controlled anti-seizure medication withdrawal to characterize seizure patterns and assess surgical candidacy. Subjects included 5 males (ages 3-22) and 17 females (ages 1.5-19). Signals were sampled at 256 Hz using the International 10-20 electrode system with bipolar montages, and have been converted to BIDS format with standardized channel nomenclature and de-identified subject information.
methods_description: Subjects were monitored continuously with scalp EEG following anti-seizure medication withdrawal in a hospital setting. EEG signals were recorded at 256 samples per second with 16-bit resolution using the International 10-20 electrode system in bipolar montage configuration. Most recordings contained 23 EEG channels; additional signals including ECG and vagal nerve stimulus were recorded in some cases. Recording files typically contained one hour of continuous data, with some cases containing two-hour or four-hour recordings. The original .edf files from PhysioNet have been converted to BIDS format with standardized channel nomenclature and de-identified subject information.
